description: Multi-replica Strata cluster (lab profile). Two TiKV-backed strata replicas behind nginx LB at port 9999, sharing JWT secret via docker volume. Adds a `lab-tikv` compose profile, a smoke shell script driving 3 failure scenarios, and a gated Playwright e2e job (triggered by [multi-replica] in PR title or workflow_dispatch). Closes the leader_for-chip gap (Supervisor lease state was never wired into the heartbeat row). 7 stories. Closes ROADMAP P3 'Multi-replica lab (TiKV)' on cycle close.

userStories.title: Wire Supervisor lease state to Heartbeater (leader_for chip)
userStories.description: As an operator, I want the Cluster Overview's leader_for chip to reflect the actual lease holder per worker so worker-rotation in a multi-replica deployment is observable from the console.
acceptanceCriteria: cmd/strata/workers.Supervisor exposes a notification channel / callback emitting (workerName string, acquired bool) on every lease state transition (acquire on runOnce enter, release on runOnce exit). Channel is buffered cap 8 so a transient consumer stall never blocks supervision.
internal/heartbeat.Heartbeater exposes SetLeaderFor(worker string, owned bool) mutating Heartbeater.Node.LeaderFor under a mutex; next write tick picks up the new slice.
internal/serverapp.runApp wires the channel: a goroutine consumes Supervisor.LeaderEvents() and calls hb.SetLeaderFor on each event; goroutine exits on channel close (supervisor shutdown).
Unit test in cmd/strata/workers/supervisor_test.go covers single-worker run that acquires + releases + verifies events fire in order.
Unit test in internal/heartbeat/heartbeat_test.go covers SetLeaderFor adding + removing entries; Run picks up the change at the next tick.

ROADMAP gets a NEW entry under ## Web UI: ~~**P3 — Heartbeat 'leader_for' chip wired to actual lease state.**~~ — **Done.** ... close-flip in this same commit per CLAUDE.md 'Discovering a new gap' + 'Closing a roadmap item' rules.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: lab-tikv compose profile with 2 strata replicas
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a lab-tikv compose profile that brings up 2 TiKV-backed strata replicas (strata-tikv-a, strata-tikv-b) so the multi-replica scenario is reproducible from one command.
acceptanceCriteria: deploy/docker/docker-compose.yml adds two services strata-tikv-a + strata-tikv-b under profile lab-tikv; both extend the existing strata-tikv shape (image, env, depends_on pd+tikv+ceph) but pin distinct host ports (9001 + 9002) and distinct STRATA_NODE_ID (strata-a / strata-b).

Both replicas inherit identical STRATA_STATIC_CREDENTIALS, STRATA_AUTH_MODE, STRATA_TIKV_PD_ENDPOINTS, STRATA_WORKERS from the host env (different creds across replicas would 403 cross-replica SigV4).
Profile activated via 'docker compose --profile lab-tikv up -d pd tikv ceph strata-tikv-a strata-tikv-b strata-lb-nginx prometheus grafana' (single command).
New Makefile target up-lab-tikv mirrors up-tikv but uses the lab profile + LB.
New Makefile target wait-strata-lab polls http://127.0.0.1:9999/readyz (LB) until 200, plus polls the two direct ports (9001 + 9002) for direct-replica readiness.
Both replicas pass /readyz after pd+tikv+ceph healthy.
Verify compose YAML syntax: docker compose --profile lab-tikv config -q exits 0.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: nginx LB container at port 9999
userStories.description: As an operator, I want a single host port (9999) routing requests to both replicas so clients have one URL regardless of replica count.
acceptanceCriteria: New compose service strata-lb-nginx under profile lab-tikv, image nginx:1.27-alpine, host port 9999 -> container 80.
deploy/nginx/strata-lab.conf (new) defines an upstream { least_conn; server strata-tikv-a:9000; server strata-tikv-b:9000 max_fails=2 fail_timeout=10s; ... } block.

proxy_pass http://strata; preserves Host, X-Forwarded-For, X-Forwarded-Proto. STREAMING-FRIENDLY settings (REQUIRED for multipart uploads): proxy_request_buffering off, proxy_buffering off, client_max_body_size 0, proxy_read_timeout 300s, proxy_send_timeout 300s, proxy_http_version 1.1.
aws --endpoint-url http://127.0.0.1:9999 succeeds against the LB (reaches one of the two replicas).
CI shell test asserts config file syntax: docker run --rm -v ./deploy/nginx/strata-lab.conf:/etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf nginx:1.27-alpine nginx -t exits 0.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Shared JWT secret via atomic file bootstrap
userStories.description: As an operator, I want the console JWT secret to be shared between replicas so a session cookie issued by replica A is accepted by replica B.
acceptanceCriteria: New named docker volume strata-jwt-shared in docker-compose.yml, mounted into both replicas at /etc/strata/jwt-shared (read-write, dir mode 0700).

internal/serverapp.loadJWTSecret extends to consult /etc/strata/jwt-shared/secret AFTER STRATA_CONSOLE_JWT_SECRET env + STRATA_JWT_SECRET_FILE, BEFORE the ephemeral fallback. Final precedence: env > STRATA_JWT_SECRET_FILE > /etc/strata/jwt-shared/secret (NEW) > ephemeral.

Bootstrap is FILE-BASED, NOT lock-based (no leader.Session; locker isn't built when loadJWTSecret runs). Use os.OpenFile(path, O_WRONLY|O_CREATE|O_EXCL, 0600). Exactly one writer wins per POSIX; loser's O_EXCL returns EEXIST and falls through to a read of the now-existing file. Re-read up to 3 times with 100 ms backoff to absorb the fsync race window.
When STRATA_CONSOLE_JWT_SECRET env is set, the shared-file path is NOT touched (avoids surprising file write on first boot of an env-configured replica).

New file internal/serverapp/jwt_secret_test.go exercises: empty dir / single replica writes file; empty dir / two concurrent calls — one writes, one reads, both return identical 32 bytes; pre-existing file returned verbatim.
Login on http://127.0.0.1:9999 (LB) then refresh — session sticky regardless of which replica handles the refresh.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Lab smoke script for multi-replica scenarios
userStories.description: As a developer, I want one shell script that drives the three acceptance scenarios end-to-end so I can validate the lab without Playwright.
acceptanceCriteria:
New scripts/multi-replica-smoke.sh runs against 'make up-lab-tikv'. Drives:
      (a) curl http://127.0.0.1:9999/admin/v1/cluster/nodes (after login) -> assert 2 nodes both healthy, exactly one carries lifecycle-leader chip and exactly one carries gc-leader chip.
      (b) docker stop strata-tikv-a -> sleep 35 -> curl -> assert 1 node remains healthy + the surviving replica carries BOTH leader chips.
      (c) docker start strata-tikv-a -> sleep 30 -> curl -> assert 2 nodes healthy again.
      (d) PUT object via replica-a (9001 direct), GET via replica-b (9002 direct) -> assert byte-equal payload.
      (e) Identify which replica holds lifecycle-leader -> kill that container -> wait 35 s -> assert OTHER replica row now carries lifecycle-leader chip.
Login flow at script start: POST /admin/v1/auth/login with STRATA_STATIC_CREDENTIALS access-key, capture session cookie, reuse for the rest of the curls.
Exit code 0 on all green; non-zero with descriptive 'echo "FAIL: <scenario>"' on any failure.
make smoke-lab-tikv Makefile target invokes the script.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass (script itself is the test; CI gates on [multi-replica] PR title — see US-006)

userStories.title: Playwright multi-replica.spec.ts e2e (gated CI job)
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want Playwright e2e for multi-replica failure scenarios so regressions surface in CI when the lab profile changes — but gated so the default fast e2e job stays under 5 min.
acceptanceCriteria: New web/e2e/multi-replica.spec.ts with three tests: cluster-overview-shows-2-nodes (login -> Cluster Overview -> assert 2 healthy rows), cross-replica-put-get (login -> upload via console -> reload -> assert visible), worker-rotation (identify lifecycle-leader holder -> dockerStop -> wait 35s -> reload -> assert OTHER replica carries the chip).
web/e2e/fixtures/docker.ts (new) exports dockerStop(name) / dockerStart(name) wrapping child_process.execFile('docker', ...). No mocking — tests assume real docker.

NEW dedicated CI job e2e-ui-multi-replica in .github/workflows/ci.yml: triggered ONLY when PR title contains [multi-replica] OR via workflow_dispatch (manual trigger). Steps: checkout -> setup-go -> setup-node -> pnpm install -> playwright install -> make up-lab-tikv -> make wait-strata-lab -> pnpm exec playwright test multi-replica.spec.ts -> make down. Timeout 15 min total. Uploads playwright-report/ artifact on failure.

NO change to the existing e2e-ui job — multi-replica.spec.ts is excluded via testIgnore in the default playwright project; the gated job uses a separate CLI flag (--project=multi-replica or a separate config).
Multi-replica spec uses NO inline webServer block — instead baseURL: http://127.0.0.1:9999 pointing at the docker-compose-managed nginx LB.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill (manual replay of three scenarios)

userStories.title: docs/multi-replica.md + ROADMAP close-flip + cycle-end merge
userStories.description: As an operator, I want a documented runbook for the multi-replica lab AND the ROADMAP entry flipped so the cycle is fully closed.
acceptanceCriteria: New docs/multi-replica.md covers: why replicas (HA, no quorum among gateways themselves); minimum count (1 = SPOF, 2 = HA, 3+ optional); JWT secret distribution model (file-based atomic bootstrap, env override); LB wiring; expected behaviour under each failure scenario; how internal/leader.Session rotates worker leadership; how Heartbeater.SetLeaderFor (US-001) reflects rotation in the UI within the next heartbeat tick (~10 s).

ROADMAP gets new entry: ~~**P3 — Multi-replica lab (TiKV).**~~ — **Done.** <one-line summary>. (commit `<sha>`) under ## Web UI or ## Developer experience. Close-flip lands in the same commit as the work per CLAUDE.md 'Closing a roadmap item' rule.
